text: 1. WEALTH-WINNER, dwelling in the sky, bringing us vigour with the juice, Flow to the filter when effused. 2. So, in thine ancient ways, may he, beloved, with a thousand streams Run o'er the fleecy straining-cloth. 3. Him who is like a caldron shake: O Indu, shake thy gift to us Shake it, armed Warrior! with thine arms. 4. Indu, invoked with many a prayer, bring down the vigour of these men, Of him who threatens us with war. 5. Indu, Wealth-giver, with thine help pour out for us a hundred, yea, A thousand of thy pure bright streams.

metadata.title: Rig Veda - Mandala 9 Hymn 52
metadata.citation: Rig Veda, Mandala 9, Hymn 52; Ralph T. H. Griffith translation
